skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Search the web and extract page content through the inference.sh CLI using Tavily and Exa apps.
skill.summary: Run Tavily and Exa web search, answer, and extraction workflows from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code.
skill.author: skillssh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, URL, and weak-cryptography patterns. Manual review found no malicious intent, no credential theft, and no prompt injection, but the skill intentionally uses an external CLI and third-party network services. Publish with a clear warning that user queries and URLs may be sent through inference.sh, Tavily, and Exa.

medium_findings.title: Broad Infsh Command Permission
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash commands matching infsh *. This is required for the skill, but it grants a broad external command surface through the inference.sh CLI.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission is explicit and semantically relevant. The risk is medium because the command scope is broad, although limited to the infsh binary.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Web Requests
medium_findings.description: The skill sends search queries and extraction URLs to inference.sh apps backed by Tavily and Exa. This is expected behavior, but users should avoid sensitive data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly describes web search and URL extraction through external services. I found no evidence of hidden exfiltration beyond the stated purpose.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as Ruby Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution across Markdown examples and table entries. These lines are documentation, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows Markdown code fences and inline code markers, not Ruby source execution. The real command risk is captured separately through the allowed infsh command.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithms, but the cited lines contain metadata, table headers, or documentation links. No cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations do not call or configure MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or another weak algorithm. No evidence of cryptographic code was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on infsh app run commands. This is legitimate for the tool, but it executes external commands from the AI environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command permission and examples are explicit. The pattern is dangerous only if inputs are sensitive or the infsh account has privileged access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Content Submission
dangerous_patterns.description: Search terms and extraction URLs are sent to external services. This creates privacy risk for confidential research topics or private URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is the core feature and is documented clearly. No evidence found of covert endpoints or unrelated data collection.

content.value_statement: Teams need current web context and clean page text for research tasks. This skill runs Tavily and Exa search or extraction through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Tavily Search Assistant queries through the infsh CLI.
Extracts clean text and images from URLs using Tavily Extract.
Runs Exa search for relevant links with context.
Uses Exa Answer for direct factual responses.
Extracts and analyzes web page content through Exa Extract.
Combines search results with LLM summarization workflows.
limitations: Requires a working inference.sh CLI installation and login.
Sends search queries and URLs to external services.
Quality depends on Tavily, Exa, and source page availability.
Does not verify every source without additional review.

use_cases.title: Product Research
use_cases.description: Collect recent market links, competitor pages, and summaries before planning product work.

use_cases.title: RAG Source Collection
use_cases.description: Find and extract web pages that can support retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.

use_cases.title: Claim Verification
use_cases.description: Search for source material and extract pages that help validate factual claims.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Web Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Find current sources for a simple topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for recent information about [topic]. Return the most useful sources and a short summary.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Article Content
prompt_templates.scenario: Pull clean text from a known page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the main content from this URL: [URL]. Summarize the key points and list any source images.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Search Providers
prompt_templates.scenario: Use both Tavily and Exa for broader coverage.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search [topic] with Tavily and Exa. Compare the strongest sources and note disagreements or missing context.

prompt_templates.title: Build Research Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a sourced research brief from multiple searches.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [question] using search and extraction. Produce a brief with source links, evidence quality, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Search for best practices for building AI agents.
output_examples.output: A concise answer with several source links, short source summaries, and suggested follow-up topics.

output_examples.input: Extract the main content from a long article URL.
output_examples.output: Clean page text, detected images, and a short summary of the article's main points.

output: A ranked source list with publication context.
A trend summary based on the collected sources.

best_practices: Use precise queries with dates, entities, and source types.
Avoid sending confidential prompts, private URLs, or credentials.
Review cited pages before relying on high-impact claims.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated answers as verified facts without sources.
Do not extract pages that require private access or contain sensitive data.
Do not use broad searches when a focused query would reduce noise.

faq.question: What services does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses inference.sh CLI apps for Tavily Search, Tavily Extract, Exa Search, Exa Answer, and Exa Extract.

faq.question: Does it need an account?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples require the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated infsh session.

faq.question: Can it access current web information?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to run web search and extraction through external search providers.

faq.question: Can I use it for private URLs?
faq.answer: Avoid private or sensitive URLs unless your organization approves sending them to the configured external services.

faq.question: Does it replace human fact-checking?
faq.answer: No. It helps gather sources, but important claims still need source review and verification.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
